raw_consumer_statement:
In XX/XX/year>, I was fraudulently induced into charging {$91000.00} total across multiple American Express accounts to merchant XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Merchant ID : XXXX, XXXX WY ). The merchant, operating under XXXX XXXX XXXX, presented a signed contract ( executed XX/XX/year>XXXX  ) guaranteeing repayment of {$80000.00} by XX/XX/year>. The merchant never repaid. This is not a speculative investment it is a guaranteed repayment contract where the merchant kept the funds. 
I filed fraud claims with American Express. They were denied on the basis that the transactions constituted " investment/securities '' activity. This classification is incorrect. The signed contract contains an explicit repayment guarantee with a fixed deadline and a $ XXXX penalty for non-payment. A guaranteed repayment obligation is not an investment. 
Additionally, an agent of the merchant admitted in writing that services were never fulfilled. The same agent offered to create fabricated invoices to defeat chargeback claims. Any documentation the merchant submitted during the dispute process should be treated as potentially fraudulent. 
This merchant is currently under active investigation by the FBI ( IC3 ), FTC, IRS, and SEC. The merchant has blocked my contact attempts, making direct resolution impossible. 
American Express has failed to properly investigate a documented fraud scheme under active federal investigation and has declined to reopen the claims despite this evidence.
